response.description_long:
When a restaurant closes or renovates, its equipment traditionally moves through auctioneers and local dealers at steep discounts — or sits in storage. Buyers, meanwhile, face a used market with no condition standards and no delivery. Forkfleet puts both sides on one platform: a marketplace for used restaurant equipment.

Sellers photograph equipment in the Forkfleet app and get a price estimate from comparable sales. A Forkfleet inspector verifies condition — burners, compressors, door seals, temperature calibration — before the listing goes live with a graded condition report. Buyers see the report, financing options, and a delivery quote. Forkfleet coordinates freight through partner carriers, including liftgate delivery and old-equipment haul-away.

Every purchase carries a 30-day working guarantee. Forkfleet charges sellers a commission of 8 percent per sale and buyers a flat service fee; financing is offered through a lending partner. Nothing is consigned to a warehouse — equipment ships directly from seller to buyer.

Since launching in Chicago in 2019, Forkfleet has expanded to 12 metro markets and lists over 5,000 items at any time. The company is 45 people, headquartered in Chicago, IL.
